Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
This letter will serve as a reguest for a formal opinion by
the Fair Political Practices Cornrnission as to the potential
conflict of interest by supervisor Ed Mclaughlin in voting on the
apptication for Use Permit by my client, Site Constructors, Inc. f
hile been authorized by Supervisor Ed Mclaughlin, through Neil
McCabe, Deputy County Counsel for the County of Butte, to seek this
opinion to attempt to resolve this issue.
FACTUAI. ST'XXARI
Site Constructors, Inc. has made application before the Butte
County BoarC of Supervisors for a Use Permit to enlarge tl.reir use
of c6nunercial pr6perty located in Butte County, - California'
Supervisor nd Mcil,aughliln proposes to vote on the application for
tnl use Permit. focated dlrectly across the street from the
property owned by Site Constructori, Inc. ( approximately 60 feet

for backhoe services performed as an independant contractor on the

According to the enclosed exercise of option, the Meline and
Rabo families have exercised their option to purchase the subject
real property, effective December 2, 1991. The granting, or
denial, of the Use Permit on the proposed parcel of property owned
by Site Constructors, Inc. wiil have no monetary effect on the
parcel of property currently owned by Hester Patrick and leased by
Meline and Rabo. Further, the grant or denial of the Use Permit
will have no financial effect on the assets or business of the
Meline and Rabo families.
OUESTIOI{S PRESEIITED
1. Will a vot€ by Supervisor Ed l,tclaughlin ou the application
for a Use Per.[it on the proPerty of Site Constructors, rnc.
constitute a cooflict of iDterest due to his contiuuiag as an
independent coatractor on adj acent laods Ieaaed by l{e1ine and Rabo,
before tbe option date of Decenber 2' 1997?
2. Ilil1 the opinion aB to Question llo. 1 above change aftor
Decenber 2, L997 wheu tr{eline and Rabo becomo owaera of tho eubject
r€al ProPerty?
AlrAr..vsrs As ro ouEsrro[ llo. 1
It must first be determined whether Supervisor Mclaughlin's
economic interest is directly involved and whether the effect of
that clecisicn is material puisuant to Title 2- California Code of
Regulations S18702.1 (a11 further citations to the California Code
of Regulations shall ie to Tit1e 2. ) (S1S7021a) ) Since- Supervisor
Mclaughlin has no interest whatsoever in Site Constuctors, Ir:c',
his edonomic interest is not directly involved. Therefore, it must
be determined whether the effect of Supervisor Mclaughlin's
decision is material under the appropriate regulations of ss18702.2
through L8702.6 (5187021a1 1
While Meline and Rabo are a direct source of income to
Sup"r"i--r plclaughlin, their interests_ are not directly involved in
a tecision befofe his agency, therefore Supervisor Mclaughlin's
Jecision does not involv"i hiJ economic interests directly involved
in the decision. (518702.1(a) (1). )
The effect of the decision will not increase or decrease the
gross ;;venues for a fiscal year of Meline or Rabo by $10,000'00 or




Fair Political Practices Couurission
RE: Site constructors, Inc.
October 2L, 1-99l
Page 3
more; will not result in their incurring or avoiding additional
expenses or eliminating existing expenses in a fiscal year in an
amount of 52r500.00 or; will not result in the increase or decrease
in value of assets or liabilities 
of S10,000.00 or more.
(S18702.2(sl .l
Section 18702.3 is currently inapplicable as Supervisor
Mcl,aughlin has no direct, indirect or beneficial ownership interest
in the real property leased by Meline and Rabo.
The decision will not materially affect the leasehold interest
of Meline and Rabo in that:
(a) The decision will not change legally allowab1e use
of the lease property.
(b) It is not reasonably foreseeable that the lessee
will 
change the actual use of the Property as a result
of the decision.
(c) ft is not reasonably foreseeable that the decision
will result in a change in the actual property within 300
feet of the teased property, and the changed use wiLl not
significantly enhance or significantly decrease the use
or enjoyment of the leased Property.
(d) 
The decision will not increase or decrease the
amount of rent for the leased property by $250.00 or 5t,
whichever is greater, during any 12 month period
following the decision.
(e) 
The decision witl not result in a change in the
termination of the lease. (S18702.4.)
To the extent that Meline and Rabo can be considered
individuals, s18702.6 is inapplicable in that the decision will not
affect their lncome, investirLnts or other tangible or intangible
"=="t. by $1r000.00; or the decisi,on will not affect the real
pi"p".ty' 
interests, 
as previously stated under 518702'4'
( S18702.6. )
Based upon this analysis, it is concluded that Supervisor
Mclaughlin ha-s no conflict -of interest and may prope':Iy vot-e on the
propo;ed land Use Permit submitted by Site Constructors, Inc"




Fair Pol-itical Practices Commission
RE: Site Constructors, Inc.
October 21, l99L
Page 4
AIIALISIS OF OUESTIOT IIO. 2
The only facts that have changed with regard to Question No.
2 is that Meline and Rabo will become owners of the subject real
property after December 2, 1991. AlI of the previous factors
remain the same, except the application of S18702.3, after December
2, 1991, needs to be considered. Since Supervisor Ed Mclauglin has
no direct, indirect or beneficial ownership interest in the real
property to be owned by Meline and Rabo, 518702.3 is indirectly
inapplicable. Horrever, there exists the possibility that it could
be applicable by virtue of S18702.6(b) in that the decision will
effect the individuals real property interest in a manner
considered material- under S18702.3. As indicated above, Meline and
Rabo are farming businesses, and should not be considered
individuals in this transaction. 
Hor^rever, even if 
they are
considered individuals, there is no showing under 518702.3(a) (t-)
that the decision will have any financial effect upon the real
property interest of Meline and Rabo.
Therefore, it is concluded that after December 2, 199L that
Supervisor Ed Mclaughlin will also not have a conflict of interest
and may properly vote on the proposed Use Permit by Site
Constructors, Inc.
It is hereby requested that your office confirm this analysis,
by formal writing, within the statutory period of time. 
We are
hoping to get this matter on for vote and consideration by
Supervisor Mclaughlin as soon as possible. If possible during the
frimework of current assignments, it would be appreciated if this
matter could be expedited as soon as possible.
ttention to this matter.
. BARRETTE

DAVID M. MCCLAIN

october 25, L99!
State of California
Fair Political Practices commission
P. O. Box 807
sacramento, CA 95812
Attn: scott Hallabrin, Acting General counsel
Re: site constructors, Inc.
Application for use Permit
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
This office has received a copy of the letter to you from
Michael Barrette, Attorney at Law, dated October 2L, 1991. 
We
would like to comment on the letter as fol-Lows.
The appJ-icant for the use permit in question sought the perrnit
to expand i non-conforming use. The county Planning Commission
denied the pernit, and the applicant appealed to the Board of
Supervisors .
The appeal caroe before the Board of SuPervisors at a reqular
rneeting on- September !7, lggL. Four Board members participated in
the heiring; ttre tj.tttr, Supervisor Ed McLaughlin, absented hirnself
because of a perceived potential conflict of interest. (Please see
enclosed lettLr of October 8, 1991 frour William H. Randolph, Chief
Administrative Officer 
(and also cLerk of 
the Board of
Supervisors). Said letter is addressed to Howard s. Horn, who is
the President of Site constructors Inc. )
As indicated in said J-etter, at the conclusion of the Board of
supervisors hearing a motion was made and seconded to uphold the
apiea:,; however, tlie notion did not pass since the vote on it $/as
a- Z-Z ii". 
The result was that the Planning Cornmission denial of
the project becarne the final decision.
Site Constructors Inc. is seeking a recons j'deration of the
matter by the Board of supervisors with supervisor McLaughlin


participating so that the tie can be broken. Title 2, California
code of Regulations section 187o1(c) (1) precludes a disqualified
member fron participating to break a tie. 
Therefore, in order for
Supervisor McLaughlin to participate it lrould be necessary to
conclude that his perceived potentiaf conflict is not an actual
conflict and he is not precluded from participating.
we have advised Supervisor McLaughlin not to participate
unless a letter is received frorn you expressing the opinion that
there is no conflict. 
Supervisor llclaughlin has followed this
advice .
As Mr. Barrette has pointed out, the Use Permit Application
does not directly affect Supervj-sor Mclaughlin. The question is
the affect, if any, on a source of his j.ncome. Would the expansion
of a non-conforming use by Site Constructors, Inc. have an affect
upon the source of supervisor ucLaughlin's income, which source
currently leases property directly 
across the street and has
exercised an option to purchase the leased property?
Thank you for your attention to these matters.
very truly yours,
SUSAN II{INAS IAN
Butte county counsel
Assistant county counsel

MEMBERS OF THE BOABO:
HASKEL A. McINTURF
JANE OOLAN
KEVIN CAMPBELL
ED McLAUGHLIN
LEN FULTON
October 8, 1991
Howard S. Horn
P.O. Box 654
Chico, CA 95965
Re: Use Permit, AP 04G06G067
Dear Mr. Horn:
At the regular meeting of the Butte County Board of Supervisors on September L7, L99L,
your appeal of the Planning Commission's denial of a IJse Permit to allow the expansion
of a nonconforming use to allow the pre-fabrication and storage of hydro-electric
equipment was heard. Four Board members participated in the hearing; the fifth
member absented himself because of a perceived potential conllict of interest.
At the conclusion of the hearing a motion was made and seconded to uphold the appeal;
however, the motion did not pass since the vote on it was a 2-2 lie. No further motion
was made. Therefore, the result of the Board proceedings was that no action, either to
uphold or deny the appeal, was taken by the Board, and the Commission's denial of the
project is the final decision on the projecl
Should you have any questions regarding this matter, please contact the Planning
Department between 10:00 a-m. and 3:00 p.m.
Very truly yours,
,,1
Llrtun,,-' fr ho^utSl-
William H. Randolph r \
Chief Administrative Offi cer

Yuba City, CA 95993

sections.question:
1. Will a vote by Supervisor Ed Mclaughlin on the application for a Use Permit on the property of Site Constructors, Inc. constitute a conflict of interest due to his continuing as an independent contractor on adjacent lands leased by Meline and Rabo, before the option date of December 2, 1991?

2. Will the opinion as to Question No. 1 above change after December 2, 1991 when Meline and Rabo become owners of the subject real property?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Supervisor Ed McLaughlin has a conflict of interest in voting on a use permit for Site Constructors, Inc., given his income from backhoe work performed on adjacent property leased (and later to be owned) by the Meline and Rabo families.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request letter dated October 21, 1991, supplemented by supporting documents (option exercise notice, letter from Hester Patrick, and County Counsel response dated October 25, 1991). The attorney-drafted request includes the requester's own legal analysis and cites specific regulations, which is atypical for an incoming request. The FPPC's actual response/opinion is not included in this document package.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Will a vote by Supervisor Ed Mclaughlin on the application for a Use Permit on the property of Site Constructors, Inc. constitute a conflict of interest due to his continuing as an independent contractor on adjacent lands leased by Meline and Rabo, before the option date of December 2, 1991?

2. Will the opinion as to Question No. 1 above change after December 2, 1991 when Meline and Rabo become owners of the subject real property?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from attorney Michael Barrette, authorized by Supervisor Ed McLaughlin, seeking an FPPC formal opinion on whether the Supervisor has a conflict of interest in voting on a use permit application. The letter includes detailed factual analysis and cites California Code of Regulations sections to argue no conflict exists before or after December 2, 1991.
